translation: Q3 has ḥataf pataḥ. Other MSS and MAM agree: shewa, no ga'aya. Prints add ga'aya.
LC missing ḥataf dots in het (pataḥ instead of ḥataf pataḥ). Typist corrected silently.
Question whether AC(S) has ḥataf pataḥ in shin. Answer: no, shewa only, no meteg in vav.
Question whether AC(S) has meteg, ḥataf pataḥ, and segol. Answer: no meteg, shewa only in het, tsere in mem.
Typist noted unusual form. Possibly ḥataf pataḥ as in Venice MG (corrected in MS). Noted in BHS.
LC has shewa instead of ḥataf in ayin.
AC(S) and LC confirm: no ḥataf in resh.
LC missing ḥataf dots in ayin. Prints have maqaf-joined word with ga'aya.
LC surprisingly has ḥataf dots in het and right ga'aya (MAM has plain pataḥ).
LC surprisingly has right ga'aya and ḥataf dots in het (MAM has plain pataḥ).
AC(S) etc: ḥataf pataḥ with ga'aya. LC missing ḥataf dots in interrogative he. Prints: ḥataf pataḥ, no ga'aya.
LC has shewa in het instead of ḥataf pataḥ.
Discussion of ga'aya placement before segol + ḥataf segol. Various MS readings differ on ga'aya position.
LC has ḥataf pataḥ in he (MAM has pataḥ). Typist did not note this.
LC has ḥataf pataḥ in ayin (MAM has pataḥ).
Question: ḥataf pataḥ in shin? Answer: no, shewa only. No ḥataf.
LC missing ḥataf dots in het.
LC has ḥataf qamats marking qamats qatan. Hataf dots clearly visible in color photo. Breuer concurs. Dotan and typist read plain qamats.
LC has ḥataf pataḥ in het (MAM has plain pataḥ).
LC has ḥataf qamats, no dagesh in shin. Venice MG also lacks dagesh. MAM follows MS et al.
LC missing ḥataf dots in alef (MAM has ḥataf segol).
Dotan uncertain about LC reading in alef — possibly ḥataf, with an erasure mark.
LC has ḥataf pataḥ in ayin (MAM has plain pataḥ).
LC has ḥataf pataḥ in vav (MAM has shewa).
LC missing ḥataf dots in alef (MAM has ḥataf pataḥ).
LC may have ḥataf qamats instead of ḥataf pataḥ in alef. Hard to discern in photo; possibly a stain.
LC and L3 missing ḥataf qamats dots in dalet.
LC may have ḥataf ḥiriq per typist. Dotan and Breuer printed ḥiriq+ga'aya without comment. Mark may be ga'aya.
LC missing ḥataf dots in alef (MAM has ḥataf pataḥ).
LC ketiv missing ḥataf dots in ayin.
LC missing ḥataf dots in het.

target_word: הַהַהגה/ג לְיְהֹוָה֙בספרי תימן הַֽלְיהֹוָה֙הַֽלְיהֹוָה֙ה.....גה/ג בתיבה אחתאאא*
translation: Complex multi-source discussion of הַלְיהֹוָה. LC has ḥataf pataḥ in he as separate word. L1 has ḥataf pataḥ in yod. Various readings.
AC has ḥataf pataḥ in both doubled letters (bet, nun). MAM and other MSS have no ḥataf, no ga'aya.
AC surprisingly has ḥataf pataḥ in alef.
AC surprisingly missing ḥataf dots in ayin. MAM, LC, and prints have ḥataf pataḥ.
AC surprisingly missing ḥataf dots in alef.
AC and LC have shewa. Some prints/Koren: ḥataf qamats. Q: ḥataf pataḥ. MS debated all three options. MG: left shin dot.
AC has ḥataf qamats marking qamats qatan. See Radak and Minchat Shai.
LC surprisingly has ḥataf pataḥ in ayin (MAM has plain pataḥ).
Per Breuer, AC has ḥataf in non-guttural letter (bet). But may be a stain, or ḥataf pataḥ with pataḥ line erased.
AC has ḥataf qamats marking qamats qatan. Also in first-edition MG and MGH.
Per Breuer, AC has ḥataf qamats for qamats qatan. Qamats visible; possible faded ḥataf dots.
AC has ḥataf qamats marking qamats qatan. Also in MGH.
AC surprisingly has segol instead of ḥataf segol in alef. MGH concurs.
LC missing ḥataf qamats dots in alef (has plain qamats instead).
Discussion of whether qamats is qatan. Radak says it should reduce. Ga'aya present. Opinions differ (Weinberg, Davidson vs. Koren, Simanim).
LC ketiv surprisingly has ḥataf pataḥ dots in ayin.
LC missing ḥataf pataḥ dots in second he.
AC has ḥataf ḥiriq in shin.
AC has ḥataf ḥiriq in tav.
Discussion of qamats qatan. MS cites ḥataf for the dagesh. Da'at Miqra: qamats qatan replacing qubuts in pu'al.
Discussion of whether qamats is gadol or qatan. Sephardi grammarians cite tradition of qamats qatan (ḥataf). MS concurs despite guttural ayin.
LC has segol in shin + ḥataf pataḥ in yod (MAM: ḥataf segol in shin + shewa in yod). Q-M: segol + ga'aya.
LC has ḥataf in qof.
AC etc: ḥataf pataḥ + ga'aya. LC has plain pataḥ in he. Breuer notes LC uses pataḥ for ḥataf in ga'aya contexts.
LC surprisingly has ḥataf segol in shin (MAM has shewa).
LC has ḥataf pataḥ in het (MAM has plain pataḥ).
System-A reading has ḥataf pataḥ. LC has ga'aya. Discussion of marka vs. ga'aya across three parallel passages.
AC has ḥataf pataḥ (as MAM). LC missing ḥataf dots in ayin.
LC may lack ḥataf pataḥ in het (shewa instead). But pataḥ line likely exists; collides with lamed in line below.
LC missing ḥataf dots in alef (MAM has ḥataf segol).
LC has phonetic ga'aya + rafe on pe (mobile shewa). Petersburg MS has ḥataf in tsade. MS notes some MSS have ḥataf in tsade.
LC etc: ḥataf qamats in dalet. Prints: shewa. Applies throughout Esther.
LC has ḥataf pataḥ dots in ayin (MAM has plain pataḥ).
LC may have ḥataf qamats in alef (MAM has ḥataf pataḥ). But dot is different color — may be a stain.
Q-M etc: ḥataf segol (as MAM). LC has ḥataf pataḥ in alef instead.
Hataf segol in gimel marks morphological segol vowel. Cf. verse 19.
LC has ḥataf pataḥ in ayin (MAM has plain pataḥ).
LC may have ḥataf pataḥ in yod. Breuer marked with doubt — smudged, odd line under yod.
LC has ḥataf qamats dots in he.
LC has ḥataf qamats in qof to mark the morphological ḥolam vowel preserved in spelling. Cf. next verse.
LC has ḥataf dots in ayin (MAM has plain pataḥ).
LC may have ḥataf pataḥ in resh. Breuer: yes. Dotan: uncertain. Examination shows no clear ḥataf line.
AC surprisingly has shewa in vav + pataḥ in he (MAM: ḥataf pataḥ in he). Possibly ḥataf dots belong to he with vav unpointed.
AC and S1 have ḥataf qamats marking qamats qatan. LC has plain qamats (as prints).
